verdict.answer_first: B states the action immediately: "flexbox on the parent — three lines"; A also answers first with "Flexbox on the parent" but adds more setup.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more cleanly: "For almost all cases, reach for flexbox first"; A is slightly more survey-like with "Other good options" and "Which to use".
verdict.fabrication: B overclaims by omitting the needed parent height and saying it centers "both axes regardless of their size"; A grounds the vertical caveat: "the parent needs a height".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to apply correctly because it includes the missing vertical requirement: "height: 100vh" and shows full HTML plus CSS.
